title: Epic: Audit and optimize code-reviewer agent
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Audit the agent definition (agents/code-reviewer.md) for prompt redundancy, over-explanation, and unnecessary verbosity
- Evaluate model choice at each of the 4 invocation sites — justify opus or identify where sonnet suffices without quality loss
- Audit context passed at each call site — strip anything the reviewer does not use to reach its verdict
- Audit tool usage in the agent — identify redundant graph lookups or searches
- Produce concrete, targeted edits with before/after rationale for each change
- Any change that carries quality risk must be documented with trade-off reasoning and left unchanged if the risk is unclear

## Success criteria
- [ ] Agent definition audited and tightened where safe
- [ ] All 4 invocation sites evaluated (execute-plan, finish-epic, fix-bug, refactor)
- [ ] Each proposed change documented with before/after rationale
- [ ] No quality regression — reviewer still catches contract drift and scope creep
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO reducing review depth or scope to cut cost (reason: quality is the hard constraint, efficiency is secondary)
- NO removing invocation sites (reason: each call serves a different scope — per-task drift vs. cross-task integration)
- NO changes without evidence of actual inefficiency (reason: premature optimization introduces risk without measurable gain)

## Durable decisions
- Quality is the hard constraint; efficiency is secondary
- Opus may stay at all sites — only downgrade with explicit justification per site
- Call sites are not removed — frequency is not the problem, per-call cost is

## Approach
Read the agent definition and all 4 invocation sites end-to-end. For each, evaluate: model choice (is opus justified?), prompt efficiency (is the system prompt tight?), context passed in (is anything unnecessary included?), and tool usage (are graph lookups redundant?). Apply edits only where the benefit is clear and the quality risk is low. Document every decision.

## Architecture
- Audit target: agents/code-reviewer.md
- Invocation sites: skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md, skills/finish-epic/SKILL.md, skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md, skills/refactor/SKILL.md
- Output: in-place edits to above files, no new files

## Design rationale

### Problem
The code-reviewer agent is dispatched frequently across the cape workflow (N+1 times per epic). Token cost and latency are the concern — not quality. The audit should find efficiency gains that are safe to make.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Audit agent and invocation sites, apply safe edits (selected)
**What:** Read every file involved, evaluate each dimension of cost, apply changes that have clear benefit and low quality risk.
**Chosen because:** Surgical — touches only what evidence shows is inefficient.

#### 2. Reduce call frequency (rejected)
**Why rejected:** Per-task and finish-epic reviews serve different scopes. Removing either risks missing contract drift or cross-task integration issues.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Benchmark data shows per-task reviews never catch anything the finish-epic review would have missed.

### Scope boundaries
**In scope:** Agent definition, 4 invocation sites — model, prompt, context, tool usage
**Out of scope:** Changing when reviews are called, removing review steps

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Reduce call frequency? | No | Scope is agent efficiency, not workflow frequency |
| Quality vs efficiency? | Quality wins | Never trade review depth for token savings |
| New files needed? | No | All edits are in-place |

title: Epic: Remove write-plan brainstorm gate
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Delete `gateWritePlan()` from `cli/src/services/hook.ts:464-475`
- Remove `write-plan` from the `gatedSkills` set (`hook.ts:370-377`) and `skillGates` map (`hook.ts:490-500`)
- Remove `cape context set brainstorm` from `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md:244-246`
- Remove `cape context clear brainstorm` from `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md:159-161`
- Update `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md` Step 1: when no design found in context, offer `cape:brainstorm` as option but acknowledge organic design is valid — do not mandate the formal skill

## Success criteria
- [ ] `gateWritePlan()` deleted and no references remain in `hook.ts`
- [ ] `write-plan` removed from `gatedSkills` set and `skillGates` map
- [ ] `cape context set brainstorm` removed from `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`
- [ ] `cape context clear brainstorm` removed from `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md`
- [ ] write-plan Step 1 language updated to acknowledge organic path
- [ ] Tests updated: brainstorm gate cases removed from `hook.test.ts` and `preToolUse.test.ts`
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO soft hook warning replacement (reason: in-skill check already handles "no design" case — a warning hook is a second, weaker layer doing the same job)
- NO `--skip-brainstorm` flag (reason: the gate is being removed entirely, not worked around)
- NO changes to other skill gates (reason: execute-plan, fix-bug, finish-epic gates are unrelated)

## Durable decisions
- In-skill check in write-plan Step 1 is the sole guardian of the "no design" invariant
- The brainstorm artifact (`brainstorm.txt`) is no longer part of the write-plan workflow
- `cape context set/clear brainstorm` commands are removed; they had no consumers beyond this gate

## Approach
Remove the deterministic hook gate that checked for `hooks/context/brainstorm.txt` before loading write-plan. The gate enforced *mechanism* (did you run the brainstorm skill?) rather than *substance* (do you have a design?), blocking users who designed organically in conversation.

The in-skill check in write-plan Step 1 — which inspects conversation context for a design summary — becomes the sole guardian. This is already present; we're removing the redundant, over-enforcing outer layer. Write-plan's validation steps (Step 2 tightens vague requirements) serve as the additional backstop.

The brainstorm artifact file and its `set`/`clear` commands in the skill files are removed as dead ceremony — nothing will check for the file after the gate is gone.

## Architecture
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts` — delete `gateWritePlan()`, remove from `gatedSkills` and `skillGates`
- `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` — remove `cape context set brainstorm` from Step 4
- `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md` — remove `cape context clear brainstorm` from Step 3; update Step 1 language
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts` — remove brainstorm gate test cases
- `cli/src/__e2e__/preToolUse.test.ts` — remove brainstorm gate test cases

## Design rationale

### Problem
The write-plan hook gate checks for a file artifact set by the brainstorm skill. Users who design organically in conversation (without running `/cape:brainstorm`) are blocked even when a valid design exists. The gate enforces workflow mechanism, not design substance.

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:370-377` — `gatedSkills` set; `write-plan` is one of six gated skills
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:464-475` — `gateWritePlan()`: reads `hooks/context/brainstorm.txt`, denies if missing
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:490-500` — `skillGates` map wiring gate to skill name
- `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md:244-246` — `cape context set brainstorm` in Step 4
- `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md:39-50` — Step 1 in-skill check (soft fallback already exists)
- `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md:159-161` — `cape context clear brainstorm` in Step 3
- `cli/src/commands/context.ts` — `cape context set/clear` writes/removes a `.txt` flag file; command itself stays

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Remove gate entirely (selected)

**What:** Delete `gateWritePlan()` and remove `write-plan` from gate infrastructure. Update skill files to remove artifact ceremony and update Step 1 language.
**Pros:** Unblocks organic brainstorming. In-skill check handles the real invariant. No redundant layers.
**Cons:** In-skill check is AI judgment, not deterministic. Acceptable tradeoff confirmed.
**Chosen because:** Gate enforces mechanism not substance; in-skill check + write-plan validation is sufficient.

#### 2. Soft hook warning (rejected)

**What:** Replace deny with `additionalContext` injection — nudge without blocking.
**Why explored:** Preserves signal that formal brainstorm wasn't run.
**Why rejected:** Two layers doing the same job. In-skill check already handles this with more context.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Evidence that in-skill check consistently fails to catch no-design cases.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** `hook.ts` gate removal, skill file cleanup, test cleanup.
**Out of scope:** Other skill gates, `cape context` command itself, any other skills.

### Open questions
- None.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Is in-skill check reliable enough to replace hook gate? | Yes — accept reliability tradeoff | No hook gate; write-plan validation is backstop |
| Should artifact commands stay in skill files? | No — remove both | `set/clear brainstorm` removed from both skills |
| Should in-skill check language update to acknowledge organic path? | Yes | Step 1 updated to offer both options |

### Dead-end paths

#### Soft hook warning
**Why explored:** Preserves gate signal without hard blocking.
**What found:** Would be a second, weaker layer on top of the in-skill check — redundant.
**Why abandoned:** YAGNI; in-skill check already handles the "no design" case.

title: Epic: classify challenge phase from flow-phase.json not conversation
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- challenge must read `hooks/context/flow-phase.json` to classify the current phase (design vs. implementation) instead of inferring from conversation context
- If `flow-phase.json` is absent or unreadable, default to "design" (most conservative depth cap)
- Remove or replace the conversation-context inference path for phase classification

## Success criteria
- [ ] challenge reads `hooks/context/flow-phase.json` in its orient/context step
- [ ] Phase classification branches on the file's content, not conversation inference
- [ ] Absent file defaults to "design" phase
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO inferring phase from conversation history (reason: conversation context is unreliable across sessions and after /clear)
- NO blocking challenge if flow-phase.json is absent (reason: design phase is a safe default)

## Durable decisions
- `hooks/context/flow-phase.json` is the canonical phase signal — written by `cape br` commands during workflow execution

## Approach
In challenge's Step 1 (context loading), add a read of `hooks/context/flow-phase.json` using the Read tool or a `cat` call. Parse the phase value. Use it to set depth cap: "design" → full depth, "implementation" / "post-task" → focused on scope creep. If absent, default to "design".

## Architecture
Single file: `skills/challenge/SKILL.md`
- Step 1 context loading: add flow-phase.json read
- Phase classification table: replace "infer from conversation" with "read from flow-phase.json"

## Design rationale

### Problem
challenge classifies the current phase (design vs. post-implementation) by reading conversation context (lines 56-64). After `/clear` or in a fresh session, conversation context is gone — challenge misclassifies and applies the wrong depth cap. `flow-phase.json` is already written by the br CLI during workflow execution and contains this information authoritatively.

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- `skills/challenge/SKILL.md:56-64` — phase classification from conversation context
- `hooks/context/flow-phase.json` — written by `cli/src/commands/br.ts` `writeFlowPhase`

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Read flow-phase.json (selected)
**What:** Replace conversation inference with file read.
**Chosen because:** File is already written by the CLI; authoritative across sessions.

#### 2. Add `cape context show-phase` CLI command (rejected)
**Why rejected:** The file is readable directly; adding a CLI wrapper is indirection without benefit.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** flow-phase.json format changes or access needs to be abstracted.

### Scope boundaries
**In scope:** `skills/challenge/SKILL.md` phase classification.
**Out of scope:** `flow-phase.json` format or write logic.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made
| Question | Answer | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| Read file directly or via CLI? | Read directly | Simpler, no new command needed |
| Absent file behavior? | Default to "design" | Conservative — full depth cap |

title: Epic: add close-check gate to fix-bug before close
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- `cape br close-check <bug-id>` must run before `cape br close <bug-id>` in Step 4
- If close-check fails, stop and report which checks failed — do not close
- Add critical rule: run `cape br close-check` before `cape br close` — checks must pass before closure

## Success criteria
- [ ] `cape br close-check <bug-id>` appears in Step 4 before `cape br close <bug-id>`
- [ ] Step 4 text states: if close-check fails, stop and report failures
- [ ] Critical rule added mirroring execute-plan's close-check pattern
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO calling `cape br close` without a preceding `cape br close-check` (reason: bug can be closed with failing checks — regression guard bypassed)
- NO skipping close-check on user approval (reason: user approval covers the fix, not the check results)

## Durable decisions
- Mirrors the execute-plan bd-5hh pattern exactly — same commands, same failure behavior

## Approach
Add `cape br close-check <bug-id>` immediately before `cape br close <bug-id>` in Step 4. If it fails, present the failures and stop — do not close. Add a new entry in `<critical_rules>` identical in structure to execute-plan's close-check rule.

## Architecture
Single file: `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md`
- Step 4 (lines 131–136): add `cape br close-check <bug-id>` before `cape br close`
- `<critical_rules>`: add rule for close-check gate

## Design rationale

### Problem
fix-bug calls `cape br close <bug-id>` (line 134) without first running `cape br close-check`. A bug can be marked fixed with failing test suite — the regression guard the skill promises is not enforced by CLI.

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md:131-136` — close sequence, no close-check call
- `skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md:144-149` — reference pattern: close-check before close

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Add close-check gate (selected)
**What:** Insert `cape br close-check <bug-id>` before `cape br close` + add critical rule.
**Chosen because:** Identical to the bd-5hh fix in execute-plan — consistent pattern across all close points.

#### 2. Rely on user approval gate (rejected)
**Why rejected:** User approval at the summary step confirms the fix looks right, not that checks are green. Separate concerns.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** `cape br close-check` is removed or subsumed into close.

### Scope boundaries
**In scope:** `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md` Step 4 and critical_rules.
**Out of scope:** debug-issue (no close step), other skills.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made
| Question | Answer | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| Mirror execute-plan exactly? | Yes | Same command sequence, same failure behavior |
| Block on user approval or check failure? | Both — checks first, then user approval | Two independent gates |

### Dead-end paths
None.

title: Task 4a: cape commit — validate conventional format, reject bulk staging, warn on sensitive files
description:
## Goal
Add cape commit <files...> -m <msg> command that validates conventional commit format, rejects bulk staging, warns on sensitive files, stages named files, and commits.

## Execution mode
AUTO

## Behaviors
- cape commit <files...> -m <msg> stages the named files and creates a git commit
- Validates message matches conventional commit format: type(scope): description or type: description
- Allowed types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore, ci, perf, build, style, revert
- Rejects bulk staging: . as a file arg is rejected with exit 1
- Warns on sensitive files matching patterns: .env*, *credentials*, *secret*, *.pem, *.key
- Sensitive file warning goes to stderr but commit still proceeds
- Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation failure
- Returns JSON to stdout: { committed: boolean, message: string, files: string[] } on success
- Returns JSON error to stderr on failure
- CommitService is an Effect service injectable via Layer for testing

## Validation rules
- Message must match: /^(feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore|ci|perf|build|style|revert)(\(.+\))?: .+$/
- At least one file must be specified
- No file arg can be . (bulk staging rejection)
- Sensitive file patterns: /^\.env/, /credentials/i, /secret/i, /\.pem$/, /\.key$/

## References
- cli/src/services/git.ts — GitService pattern
- cli/src/services/gitLive.ts — execSync pattern for git commands
- cli/src/commands/check.ts — command wiring pattern with error handling

## Success criteria
- [ ] cape commit file.ts -m "feat: add thing" stages and commits
- [ ] cape commit file.ts -m "bad message" exits 1 with validation error
- [ ] cape commit . -m "feat: bulk" exits 1 with bulk staging rejection
- [ ] cape commit .env -m "feat: add config" warns on stderr but commits
- [ ] cape commit (no files) exits 1 with error
- [ ] Returns structured JSON on success and failure
- [ ] CommitService testable via injected layer
- [ ] Pure validation functions have full test coverage
- [ ] All existing tests still passing
- [ ] vp check passes
close_reason: Implemented in 9174ed1. All success criteria met: conventional format validation, bulk staging rejection, sensitive file warnings, Effect service layer with tests, execFileSync for safe arg passing.

title: Epic: Shared scripts for cape skills and agents
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- br-append-design.ts reads existing design field via `br show --format json <id>`, appends a markdown section with `## <heading>`, writes back via `br update <id> --design`
- detect-project.ts detects language, test framework/command, linter, and formatter from CWD
- detect-project.ts supports 5 ecosystems: TypeScript, Go, Lua, Python, Rust
- detect-project.ts uses just/make targets as fallback only when no framework provides the capability
- detect-project.ts --map <dir> maps source files to test files using detected conventions
- run-checks.ts imports lib/detect.ts, runs all detected checks (tests, lint, format), returns structured JSON pass/fail with exit code
- Shared detection logic lives in scripts/lib/detect.ts, imported by detect-project and run-checks
- All scripts use #!/usr/bin/env bun, output JSON to stdout, errors to stderr
- Skills invoke scripts via bun run ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/<name>.ts

## Success criteria
- [ ] br-append-design appends sections to br issues without losing existing content
- [ ] br-append-design handles null design field (first append to empty issue)
- [ ] detect-project correctly identifies language, test framework, linter for each supported ecosystem
- [ ] detect-project --map correctly maps source files to test files
- [ ] detect-project falls back to just/make only when no framework provides the capability
- [ ] run-checks runs all detected checks and returns structured JSON pass/fail
- [ ] Scripts follow validate.ts patterns (Bun APIs, shebang, error handling)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO task runner override of framework commands (reason: framework knows its own semantics; just/make only fill gaps where no framework is detected)
- NO monorepo multi-language detection (reason: CWD scoping handles this; skills already operate in specific directories)
- NO caching of detect-project results (reason: YAGNI; re-running is cheap)
- NO separate map-tests CLI script (reason: 2 consumers don't justify a 4th entry point; --map flag suffices)
- NO taskRunner metadata when framework covers the capability (reason: gap-filling only, no redundant info)
- NO bash for new scripts (reason: structured JSON output and config parsing painful in bash; Bun is the established pattern)

## Durable decisions
- Runtime: TypeScript with Bun (#!/usr/bin/env bun), matching validate.ts precedent
- Module structure: scripts/lib/detect.ts shared by detect-project and run-checks; br-append-design standalone
- Detection scope: CWD-based, not project-root-based
- Command preference chain: Framework direct > just <target> > make <target> > absent
- Output shape: Flat JSON — one resolved "best" command per operation
- Deliverable order: br-append-design first (standalone, unblocks 4 skills), detection suite second
- Invocation convention: bun run ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/X.ts in skill bash blocks

## Approach
Build 3 TypeScript (Bun) scripts in scripts/ as shared tooling for cape skills and agents. br-append-design eliminates the error-prone 3-step pattern (br show → extract design → br update --design) repeated across 4 skills. detect-project and run-checks replace ad-hoc project/test detection prose instructions across 6+ skills and agents with structured JSON output.

Scripts follow the established validate.ts pattern: Bun shebang, git root detection, structured output. Skills reference them via explicit ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} paths, matching the hook invocation convention.

## Architecture
scripts/
├── lib/
│   └── detect.ts            # detectProject(), mapTests(), ecosystem configs
├── br-append-design.ts      # standalone: br show --json → append → br update --design
├── detect-project.ts        # CLI wrapper: imports lib, parses args, outputs JSON
├── run-checks.ts            # imports lib, runs detected commands, collects results

Output shapes:
- detect-project: { language, test: { framework, allCommand, fileCommand, filePattern, location }, lint: { command }, format: { command } }
- detect-project --map: { mappings: [{ source, test, exists, skip? }] }
- run-checks: { passed, checks: [{ name, command, passed, exitCode, output? }] }
- br-append-design: { ok, id, section }

## Design rationale

### Problem
Cape skills repeat error-prone shell patterns across 10+ skills and agents. Each re-implements br design appending (4 skills), project/test detection (6+ skills), and check execution (3+ skills) via prose instructions to Claude. This causes inconsistency, fragility, and wasted context tokens on rediscovery.

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- scripts/validate.ts — Bun shebang, Glob API, git root detection, structured output pattern
- hooks/hooks.json — bun run ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/X.ts invocation pattern
- br show --format json — returns full issue JSON with nullable design field
- br update --design <text> — replaces design field (does not append)
- br schema — emits JSON schemas for all output types
- execute-plan, fix-bug, finish-epic, expand-task — all repeat br show → extract → append → update pattern
- test-runner, TDD, analyze-tests, find-test-gaps, finish-epic, expand-task — all independently discover test framework

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Flat output with task-runner fallback (selected)

**What:** Framework commands resolved first, just/make fill gaps only. Consumers get one command per operation.
**Pros:** Simple for consumers, no decision logic needed, matches how skills actually use the output.
**Cons:** Hides task runner layer entirely when framework is detected.
**Chosen because:** Skills never need both layers simultaneously. Simplicity wins.

#### 2. Layered output (rejected)

**What:** Reports both framework and task runner detection separately. Consumers choose which to use.
**Why explored:** Transparency — consumers see full detection results.
**Why rejected:** Adds decision logic to every consumer for no benefit given task runners are fallback-only.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Task runners need to override framework commands (user explicitly rejected this).

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** 3 new scripts, 1 shared module, skill updates to reference scripts
**Out of scope:** Agent template standardization (prose-level, not scriptable), new skill creation (pr, review, lint — separate epics), existing skill rewrites beyond updating specific bash blocks

### Open questions
- Exact detection heuristics per ecosystem (discover during implementation)
- How to detect test file location convention (colocated vs tests/ directory) reliably
- Whether run-checks should capture full output or just summary + failures

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Language for scripts | TypeScript (Bun) | Matches validate.ts, structured output easy |
| Script coupling | Shared module in lib/ | 3 scripts import lib/detect.ts; br-append-design standalone |
| Skill invocation | Explicit ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} path | Zero ambiguity, matches hook convention |
| Ecosystem scope | TS, Go, Lua, Python, Rust + just/make | Detection logic cheap per-ecosystem |
| Task runner priority | Fallback only | Framework > just > make; invisible when framework detected |
| Monorepo strategy | CWD-based detection | Skills already operate in specific directories |
| map-tests | --map flag on detect-project | Function in lib, not separate CLI |
| Deliverables | Two phases | br-append-design first, detection suite second |
| Output format | Flat JSON | One resolved command per operation |

### Dead-end paths

#### Bash scripts
**Why explored:** Shell scripts are natural for CLI wrappers (br, git).
**What found:** Structured JSON output, config file parsing (package.json, Cargo.toml), and error handling are painful in bash.
**Why abandoned:** validate.ts already establishes Bun as the script runtime. Consistency and capability both favor TypeScript.

#### Separate map-tests.ts CLI
**Why explored:** analyze-tests and find-test-gaps both need source→test mapping.
**What found:** Only 2 consumers. The mapping logic is a function call, not a complex pipeline.
**Why abandoned:** --map flag on detect-project covers the need without a 4th CLI entry point.

#### Shipping all scripts as one deliverable
**Why explored:** Cohesive release.
**What found:** br-append-design has zero dependency on detection logic. It's the highest ROI script (4 skills) and simplest to build.
**Why abandoned:** Splitting lets br-append-design ship and unblock skills immediately.

### Open concerns raised
- Monorepo detection? -> CWD scoping handles it naturally
- Task runner vs framework priority? -> Framework wins, user confirmed
- Caching detect-project? -> YAGNI, deferred
close_reason: Superseded by cape-2kp (Cape CLI epic). Shared scripts approach replaced by full CLI.

title: Task 1: Build br-append-design.ts
description:
## Goal
Build scripts/br-append-design.ts — a standalone script that appends a markdown section to a br issue's design field without losing existing content.

  - Outputs: { "ok": true, "id": "<id>", "section": "## <heading>" }

title: Gate write-plan on brainstorm artifact
description:
## What
Add a PreToolUse Skill gate that blocks write-plan unless a brainstorm artifact exists on disk.

## Why
Currently write-plan only has a soft check in its SKILL.md text. A hook gate makes this enforceable — Claude cannot bypass it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Brainstorm skill writes a context file (e.g. `hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt`) when design is locked (Step 4)
- [ ] `write-plan` added to `gatedSkills` set in hook.ts
- [ ] `gateWritePlan()` checks for the brainstorm context file; denies with clear message if missing
- [ ] Gate clears (deletes) the context file after write-plan completes successfully
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Constraints
- Follow existing context file pattern (tdd-state.json, br-show-log.txt, pr-confirmed.txt)
- Fail open if filesystem check errors
- Gate function lives in hook.ts alongside other gates
design:
## Expanded plan

### Step 1: Add `gateWritePlan()` to hook.ts

**File:** `cli/src/services/hook.ts`

1. After line 456, add `'write-plan'` to `gatedSkills` set:
   ```ts
   const gatedSkills = new Set(['execute-plan', 'finish-epic', 'fix-bug', 'write-plan']);
   ```

2. Add `gateWritePlan()` function near the other gate functions (~line 483). Pattern follows `gateExecutePlan`:
   ```ts
   const gateWritePlan = () =>
     Effect.gen(function* () {
       const service = yield* HookService;
       const contextPath = `${service.pluginRoot()}/hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt`;
       const content = yield* service.readFile(contextPath);
       if (content === null) {
         return null; // fail open (file system error)
       }
       if (!content) {
         return denyWith(
           'No brainstorm artifact found. Run cape:brainstorm first to explore and lock a design.',
         );
       }
       return null;
     });
   ```

3. Add entry to `skillGates` record (~line 548):
   ```ts
   'write-plan': () => gateWritePlan(),
   ```

### Step 2: Write context file in brainstorm SKILL.md

**File:** `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`

At the end of Step 4 ("Lock design"), after the design summary is presented, add instruction to write the artifact:

```
Write the brainstorm artifact to signal completion:

\```bash
mkdir -p "$(cape plugin-root)/hooks/context" && echo "brainstorm-complete" > "$(cape plugin-root)/hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt"
\```
```

### Step 3: Clean up context file after write-plan completes

**File:** `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md`

At the end of write-plan's process (after epic creation), add instruction to clear the artifact:

```
Clear the brainstorm artifact (consumed):

\```bash
rm -f "$(cape plugin-root)/hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt"
\```
```

Also update the existing soft check (Step 1, line 39-41) to reference the hook gate as the primary enforcement.

### Step 4: Update non-gated skill list in tests

**File:** `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts`

1. Remove `'cape:write-plan'` from the `it.each` list of non-gated skills (line ~764-775).

2. Add test cases for gateWritePlan in the `preToolUseSkill` describe block:
   - Denied when no brainstorm artifact (empty file content)
   - Allowed when brainstorm artifact exists
   - Fail-open when readFile returns null

### Step 5: Verify

```bash
cd cli && npx vitest run src/commands/hook.test.ts
```

Check pre-commit hooks pass.

### Notes
- `readFile` returning `null` = fs error (fail open). Returning `''` = file missing/empty (deny).
- Context file is written by brainstorm skill (Bash in skill text), consumed+deleted by write-plan skill (Bash in skill text). The hook gate only reads.
- The `cape plugin-root` command needs to exist. Verify with `cape plugin-root` or check CLI commands.

## Expanded plan

### Step 0: Add `cape context` CLI command

**Why:** Skills need to write/clear context files but can't access `pluginRoot` from Bash. Add a thin CLI command that wraps the existing HookService file operations.

**New file:** `cli/src/commands/context.ts`
- `cape context set <name>` — writes `<pluginRoot>/hooks/context/<name>.txt` with `"true"`
- `cape context clear <name>` — removes `<pluginRoot>/hooks/context/<name>.txt`

**Modified:** `cli/src/main.ts` — register `context` subcommand.

### Step 1: Add `gateWritePlan()` to hook.ts

**File:** `cli/src/services/hook.ts`

1. Add `'write-plan'` to `gatedSkills` set (line 456)
2. Add `gateWritePlan()` function (~line 483):
   - Read `${pluginRoot}/hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt`
   - `null` (no file / error) → `denyWith('No brainstorm artifact found. Run cape:brainstorm first.')`
   - Non-null → return `null` (allow)
3. Add `'write-plan': () => gateWritePlan()` to `skillGates` record (~line 548)

Note: `readFile` returns `null` for missing file AND fs errors. Both cases deny — this is correct because "file missing" is the normal deny case. The `orElseSucceed(() => null)` in readFile already handles crashes gracefully.

### Step 2: Write context file in brainstorm SKILL.md

**File:** `skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`

At end of Step 4 ("Lock design"), after design summary, add:

```
Signal brainstorm completion for the write-plan gate:

\```bash
cape context set brainstorm-summary
\```
```

### Step 3: Clear context file in write-plan SKILL.md

**File:** `skills/write-plan/SKILL.md`

At the end of write-plan's process (after epic creation succeeds), add:

```
Clear the brainstorm artifact (consumed):

\```bash
cape context clear brainstorm-summary
\```
```

Update existing soft check (Step 1, lines 39-41) to note that the hook gate is the primary enforcement.

### Step 4: Update tests

**File:** `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts`

1. Remove `'cape:write-plan'` from `it.each` non-gated skills list (~line 764-775)
2. Add test cases for `gateWritePlan`:
   - Denied when brainstorm-summary.txt doesn't exist (`files: {}` → readFile returns null)
   - Allowed when brainstorm-summary.txt exists (`files: { '.../brainstorm-summary.txt': 'true' }`)
3. Add to fail-open test if needed (though for this gate, null = deny is intentional)

**New file:** `cli/src/commands/context.test.ts`
- Test `cape context set` writes file
- Test `cape context clear` removes file

### Step 5: Build and verify

```bash
cd cli && npm run build && npx vitest run src/commands/hook.test.ts src/commands/context.test.ts
```

Pre-commit hooks pass.

## Outcome

### Changes

1. **`cli/src/services/hook.ts`** — Added `gateWritePlan()` that reads `hooks/context/brainstorm-summary.txt`; denies write-plan if absent. Added `write-plan` to `gatedSkills` and `skillGates`.

2. **`cli/src/commands/context.ts`** (new) — `cape context set <name>` / `cape context clear <name>` CLI commands for skills to write/clear context files via `pluginRoot`.

3. **`cli/src/main.ts`** — Registered `context` subcommand.

4. **`skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md`** — Step 4 writes `brainstorm-summary` context file via `cape context set`.

5. **`skills/write-plan/SKILL.md`** — Clears `brainstorm-summary` after epic creation via `cape context clear`. Updated soft check to note hook gate as primary enforcement.

6. **Tests** — 2 new gate tests (deny/allow), 2 new context command tests, removed `cape:write-plan` from non-gated list.

### Key decision
Added `cape context` CLI command rather than having skills write files directly. Skills don't have access to `pluginRoot` from Bash — the cape CLI resolves the path.

title: Gate internal skills from direct user invocation
description:
## What
Block expand-task and test-driven-development from being invoked directly by users. These are internal skills called only by execute-plan and fix-bug.

## Why
Users invoking internal skills directly bypasses the workflow chain (brainstorm → write-plan → execute-plan → expand-task → TDD). The hook gate enforces this workflow ordering.

## Context from task 1
- `gatedSkills` set and `skillGates` record in hook.ts handle skill gates
- `preToolUseSkill()` dispatches to gate functions
- The `cape context set/clear` command exists for skills to signal state
- E2E tests in preToolUse.test.ts must also be updated

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] expand-task denied when invoked directly (no in-progress task from execute-plan)
- [ ] test-driven-development denied when invoked directly (no in-progress task from execute-plan or fix-bug)
- [ ] Both skills still work when invoked internally by execute-plan or fix-bug
- [ ] Unit and E2E tests cover deny/allow cases
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Implementation notes
The challenge: there's no field distinguishing user-invoked from internally-invoked skills. The Skill tool input is identical either way. The gate must use a heuristic — checking for an in-progress task (which exists when execute-plan is running) is the most reliable signal. execute-plan marks tasks in-progress before loading these internal skills.
design:
## Expanded plan (expand-task)

### Investigation findings

**Files to modify:**
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts` — gate infrastructure: `gatedSkills` set (L456), gate functions (L460-554), `skillGates` record (L556-564)
- `skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md` — Step 2 (L80-119): loads expand-task (L86-90) and TDD (L103)
- `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md` — Step 2-3 (L60-65): marks in_progress, Step 3 (L74-77): loads TDD
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts` — unit tests: non-gated skill list (L764-774), gate deny/allow tests (L798-932)
- `cli/src/__e2e__/preToolUse.test.ts` — E2E tests: non-gated skill list (L407-422), file-based gate pattern (L425-444)

**Patterns to follow:**
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:543-554` — `gateWritePlan()` reads a context file and denies if absent. Exact pattern for the new gate.
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts:917-932` — unit tests for write-plan gate (deny when file missing, allow when file present)
- `cli/src/__e2e__/preToolUse.test.ts:425-444` — E2E tests for write-plan gate using `writeFileSync` to set context

**Reusable code:**
- `service.readFile(path)` — reads file, returns null on error, empty string on missing file
- `denyWith(reason)` — constructs deny result
- `cape context set/clear` — CLI commands for skills to manage context files

**Blast radius:**
- execute-plan SKILL.md — new instruction to set/clear context file (additive)
- fix-bug SKILL.md — new instruction to set/clear context file (additive)
- hook.ts — new gate functions, expanded `gatedSkills` and `skillGates` (additive)
- Tests — new test cases and removal from non-gated lists

**Key design decision:**
The task description suggests using in-progress br state as the heuristic. Investigation reveals this won't work: execute-plan loads expand-task BEFORE marking the task in-progress (expand-task at L86-90, in_progress at L99-100). Instead, use a context file `workflow-active.txt` that execute-plan and fix-bug set before loading internal skills, following the established `brainstorm-summary.txt` pattern.

### Steps

### Step 1: Add `gateInternalSkill()` and register in hook.ts

**Pattern:** `cli/src/services/hook.ts:543-554` — gateWritePlan

**Changes:**
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:L456` — add `'expand-task'` and `'test-driven-development'` to `gatedSkills`
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:L555` (after gateWritePlan) — add `gateInternalSkill()` that reads `${pluginRoot}/hooks/context/workflow-active.txt`, denies if absent
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts:L560-563` — add `'expand-task'` and `'test-driven-development'` entries to `skillGates`

**Test:** Unit test: expand-task denied when workflow-active.txt absent, allowed when present. Same for TDD.

**Verify:** `cd cli && npx vitest run src/commands/hook.test.ts`

### Step 2: Update execute-plan SKILL.md to set/clear context file

**Changes:**
- `skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md:L80` — add `cape context set workflow-active` instruction before the expand-task load (before L86)
- `skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md:L119` — add `cape context clear workflow-active` instruction at task close (before/alongside `cape br close-check`)

**Test:** Manual verification that instructions are in correct order: set → expand-task → in_progress → TDD → close → clear.

**Verify:** `grep -n 'workflow-active' skills/execute-plan/SKILL.md`

### Step 3: Update fix-bug SKILL.md to set/clear context file

**Changes:**
- `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md:L60` — add `cape context set workflow-active` before loading TDD (before L76)
- `skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md` — add `cape context clear workflow-active` at bug closure

**Test:** Manual verification that set comes before TDD load.

**Verify:** `grep -n 'workflow-active' skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md`

### Step 4: Add unit tests in hook.test.ts

**Pattern:** `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts:917-932` — write-plan gate tests

**Changes:**
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts:L764-769` — remove `expand-task` and `test-driven-development` from non-gated list (if they were there — investigation shows they're not currently listed, so no removal needed, but they must NOT be added)
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts:L932` — add new describe block with 4 tests:
  1. denies expand-task when workflow-active.txt absent
  2. allows expand-task when workflow-active.txt present
  3. denies test-driven-development when workflow-active.txt absent
  4. allows test-driven-development when workflow-active.txt present

**Verify:** `cd cli && npx vitest run src/commands/hook.test.ts`

### Step 5: Add E2E tests in preToolUse.test.ts

title: Route high-confidence intents via UserPromptSubmit hook
description:
## Goal
Add a UserPromptSubmit hook that detects high-confidence user intents and routes them to the appropriate skill.

## Behaviors
- Error/stack-trace patterns in user prompt route to cape:debug-issue
- Continue/resume patterns in user prompt route to cape:execute-plan
- Ambiguous or unrecognized intents pass through without routing
- Only high-confidence matches trigger routing (false positive rate below 5 percent)

## Success criteria
- Error/stack-trace intents route to debug-issue
- Continue/resume intents route to execute-plan
- Ambiguous intents pass through without routing
- Unit tests cover match and non-match cases
- E2E tests verify routing
- Pre-commit hooks pass
design:
## Expanded plan

## Expanded plan

### Investigation findings

**Files to modify:**
- `cli/src/services/hook.ts` — Add `detectDebugIssue` and `detectExecutePlan` functions (near `detectBeadsSkill`, L47-67), integrate into `userPromptSubmit` (L160-165)
- `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts` — Add unit tests in `describe('userPromptSubmit', ...)` block (L230-286)
- `cli/src/__e2e__/protocol.test.ts` — Add E2E tests after beads detection block (L325-334)

**Patterns to follow:**
- `detectBeadsSkill` (L62-67): regex array, `.some()` match, exported for testability
- Unit test pattern (L249-256): `makeStubHookLayer({ stdin: ... })` → `Effect.runPromise(userPromptSubmit()...)` → assert `additionalContext`
- E2E test pattern (L325-334): `cape(['hook', 'user-prompt-submit'], stdin, env)` → parse stdout → assert

**Key design decisions:**
- Regex-only detection — no LLM, no external calls. High-confidence patterns only.
- Error detection: stack traces (file:line patterns, `at Function`, `Error:`, `Traceback`), explicit error reporting ("this is broken", "getting an error", "crash")
- Continue detection: explicit continuation signals ("continue", "next task", "keep going", "carry on")
- Exclusion patterns prevent false positives on ambiguous inputs ("continue this discussion", "error handling")
- Both detectors are pure functions (prompt → boolean), exported for direct unit testing

### Steps

### Step 1: Add `detectDebugIssue` and `detectExecutePlan` to hook.ts

**File:** `cli/src/services/hook.ts`

After `detectBeadsSkill` (L67), add two detector functions:

```ts
const errorPatterns = [
  /(?:^|\n)\s*at\s+\S+\s+\(.*:\d+:\d+\)/,              // JS/TS stack frame
  /(?:^|\n)\s*File ".*", line \d+/,                       // Python traceback
  /(?:^|\n)(?:panic|fatal|FATAL|PANIC):/,                 // Go/system panics
  /(?:^|\n)\S+Error:/,                                    // JS error names (TypeError:, RangeError:)
  /(?:^|\n)Traceback \(most recent call last\)/,          // Python traceback header
  /(?:getting|seeing|hitting|got|have)\s+(?:an?\s+)?error/i,  // "getting an error"
  /(?:this|it)\s+(?:is\s+)?(?:broken|crashing|failing)/i,    // "this is broken"
];

export const detectDebugIssue = (prompt: string) =>
  errorPatterns.some((pattern) => pattern.test(prompt));

const continuePatterns = [
  /^(?:continue|keep going|carry on|next task|proceed|go ahead)\.?$/i,
  /^(?:yes,?\s+)?(?:continue|keep going|carry on|next task|proceed)\.?$/i,
];

export const detectExecutePlan = (prompt: string) =>
  continuePatterns.some((pattern) => pattern.test(prompt));
```

Then integrate in `userPromptSubmit` (after L164):

```ts
if (detectDebugIssue(prompt)) {
  skills.push('cape:debug-issue');
}
if (detectExecutePlan(prompt)) {
  skills.push('cape:execute-plan');
}
```

### Step 2: Add unit tests in hook.test.ts

**File:** `cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts`

Inside `describe('userPromptSubmit', ...)` (after L285), add tests:

1. `'injects debug-issue for stack trace'` — prompt with JS stack trace
2. `'injects debug-issue for Python traceback'` — prompt with Python traceback header
3. `'injects debug-issue for explicit error report'` — "I'm getting an error"
4. `'does not inject debug-issue for error discussion'` — "how does error handling work"
5. `'injects execute-plan for continue'` — "continue"
6. `'injects execute-plan for next task'` — "next task"
7. `'does not inject execute-plan for ambiguous continue'` — "continue this discussion about APIs"

### Step 3: Add E2E tests in protocol.test.ts

**File:** `cli/src/__e2e__/protocol.test.ts`

After beads detection block (L334), add:

1. `'detects stack trace and includes cape:debug-issue'`
2. `'detects continue intent and includes cape:execute-plan'`
3. `'passes through ambiguous input without routing'`

### Step 4: Build and verify

```bash
cd cli && npm run build
npx vitest run cli/src/commands/hook.test.ts
npx vitest run cli/src/__e2e__/protocol.test.ts
```

Pre-commit hooks pass.

title: Lightweight TDD guard — soft reminder hooks that inject additionalContext when production files are edited without test evidence
description:
# Lightweight TDD guard (soft reminder)

## Requirements

1. Track test run outcomes (pass/fail) via PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure hooks on Bash
2. Detect test commands by regex matching on `tool_input.command`
3. After production code file edits, inject `additionalContext` reminders when test evidence is missing or stale (no tests run, or tests all green)
4. Stay silent when: editing test files, editing non-code files, flow-context is not executing/debugging, or tests are red (correct TDD — writing code to pass)
5. Never deny or block — only inject context
6. Clear state on fresh session startup (matching `br-show-log.txt` pattern)
7. Fail open on all errors (JSON parse, br unavailable, missing state)

## Anti-patterns

- No test output parsing — use hook event name for pass/fail
- No per-framework reporter packages
- No project-level artifacts (state lives in hooks/context/, already gitignored)
- No blocking or denying edits

## Success criteria

1. bun test passes for all new test files
2. Production file edit during executing phase with no prior test run → reminder injected
3. Production file edit during executing phase after failing test → no reminder
4. Production file edit during executing phase after passing test → reminder injected
5. Test file edit → no reminder regardless of state
6. Non-code file edit → no reminder regardless of state
7. Edit during idle/planning phase → no reminder regardless of state
8. Corrupted or missing state → no crash, no reminder (fail open)

title: PostToolUse hooks — track test outcomes and remind on production edits
description:
## Goal

Create two hooks:
1. **track-test-outcome.ts** (PostToolUse + PostToolUseFailure on Bash) — detects test commands via `isTestCommand`, writes TDD state with `writeTddState`
2. **tdd-reminder.ts** (PostToolUse on Edit) — after production code edits, injects `additionalContext` reminder when test evidence is missing or stale

## Behaviors

### track-test-outcome.ts
1. On PostToolUse/Bash: if `isTestCommand(tool_input.command)` → `writeTddState({ phase: "green", timestamp })` 
2. On PostToolUseFailure/Bash: if `isTestCommand(tool_input.command)` → `writeTddState({ phase: "red", timestamp })`
3. Non-test commands → exit 0, no state change
4. Invalid JSON input → exit 0 (fail open)

### tdd-reminder.ts
1. Read `tool_input.file_path` from stdin
2. If `isTestFile(file_path)` → exit 0 (no reminder)
3. If not `isCodeFile(file_path)` → exit 0 (no reminder)
4. Read flow-context from `deriveFlowContext` — if phase is not executing/debugging → exit 0
5. Read TDD state via `readTddState` — if phase is "red" → exit 0 (correct TDD flow)
6. If phase is "green" or state is null → output `additionalContext` reminder
7. Any error → exit 0 (fail open)

## Success criteria

- track-test-outcome: test command on PostToolUse → state written as green
- track-test-outcome: test command on PostToolUseFailure → state written as red
- track-test-outcome: non-test command → no state change
- tdd-reminder: production file edit with no test state → reminder injected
- tdd-reminder: production file edit with green state → reminder injected
- tdd-reminder: production file edit with red state → no reminder
- tdd-reminder: test file edit → no reminder
- tdd-reminder: non-code file edit → no reminder
- tdd-reminder: edit during idle/planning phase → no reminder
- tdd-reminder: corrupted state → no crash, no reminder
- hooks.json updated with both new hooks
- session-start.ts clears tdd-state.json on startup (matching br-show-log.txt pattern)

title: Test ID generation
Epic: Cape CLI — unified CLI for deterministic operations and hook dispatch
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- cape binary available in PATH via pnpm global install or symlink
- Runtime: Node (no bun dependency), bundled via Vite+ toolchain (tsdown), #!/usr/bin/env node shebang
- Built with Effect v4 (effect/unstable/cli, @effect/platform-node)
- Project setup follows greppa patterns: vite-plus, oxlint, oxfmt, vitest, pnpm
- cape detect outputs JSON with language, test framework, linter, formatter detected from CWD
- cape detect --map <dir> outputs JSON mapping source files to test files
- cape check runs detected checks (tests, lint, format), returns JSON pass/fail per check
- cape git context outputs JSON with main branch, current branch, porcelain status, diff stat, recent log
- cape commit <files...> -m <msg> validates conventional format, rejects bulk staging, warns on sensitive files, stages named files, commits
- cape pr template returns repo PR template content (cascade to bundled default)
- cape pr validate <file|--stdin> validates PR body against detected template structure
- cape br validate <id> validates existing bead structure against type-specific rules
- cape br validate --type <type> validates content from stdin before creation
- cape br design <id> <heading> reads existing design, adds markdown section, writes back
- cape validate <path|glob> validates skill, agent, and command markdown structure (frontmatter fields, required XML tags, heading variants)
- cape hook <event> [--matcher <name>] unified dispatcher, routes internally by event type
- cape hook accepts both PascalCase and kebab-case event names (PreToolUse and pre-tool-use)
- cape hook is hidden from top-level --help output (internal command for Claude Code harness)
- Hook dispatcher handles PostToolUseFailure as a distinct event type (not merged with PostToolUse)
- Hook dispatcher preserves ALL enforcement guards (see protocol spec for enumerated list)
- CLI has dual role: provide validated commands AND guard against bypass
- All commands: JSON to stdout, errors to stderr; human-readable errors when stderr is a TTY
- Exit code contract: 0 = success, 1 = validation/domain failure, 2 = usage error
- Sensitive file patterns: .env*, *credentials*, *secret*, *.pem, *.key
- Hard cutover: replaces all existing hooks/*.ts and scripts/validate.ts
- Existing hook test suites migrated + protocol-level tests
- E2E flow tests verify cross-hook state chains end-to-end (write → read → enforce sequences across hook boundaries)
- track-pr-confirmation test suite written before migration (currently zero coverage)
- Protocol module (protocol.ts) owns all 5 input interfaces and 3 output constructors
- All features implemented via strict TDD (red-green-refactor)

## Success criteria
- [ ] cape --help lists all subcommands (cape hook hidden from output)
- [ ] cape detect correctly identifies language, test framework, linter for 5 ecosystems
- [ ] cape detect --map correctly maps source files to test files
- [ ] cape check runs detected checks and returns structured JSON pass/fail
- [ ] cape git context returns valid JSON with branch, status, diff, log
- [ ] cape commit validates conventional format and rejects malformed commits
- [ ] cape commit rejects bulk staging and warns on sensitive files (.env*, *credentials*, *secret*, *.pem, *.key)
- [ ] cape pr template finds repo templates with correct cascade fallback
- [ ] cape pr validate accepts --stdin in addition to file path
- [ ] cape pr validate detects missing required sections
- [ ] cape br validate catches malformed epics, tasks, and bugs
- [ ] cape br design appends sections without losing existing content
- [ ] cape br design handles null design field
- [ ] cape validate catches malformed skill (missing frontmatter/XML tags), agent (missing headings), and command (missing body pattern) files
- [ ] cape hook session-start produces correct additionalContext output
- [ ] cape hook session-start --clear-logs truncates br-show-log and deletes tdd-state
- [ ] cape hook user-prompt-submit produces correct decision output
- [ ] cape hook user-prompt-submit injects cape:beads hint on br/beads keyword detection
- [ ] cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Bash enforces all 13 command guards
- [ ] cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Skill gates skill loading correctly
- [ ] cape hook post-tool-use handles all matchers (Bash, Edit, AskUserQuestion)
- [ ] cape hook post-tool-use-failure tracks test failures with TDD_PHASE=red
- [ ] Protocol envelope shape tests assert exact JSON keys per output type (additionalContext, decision+additionalContext, hookSpecificOutput)
- [ ] Pass-through response is empty stdout + exit 0 (never an "allow" JSON)
- [ ] E2E flow test: br show then br update --design succeeds (track-br-show writes log, enforce-commands reads it)
- [ ] E2E flow test: PR confirmation then PR creation succeeds within TTL, fails after expiry (track-pr-confirmation writes, enforce-commands reads/deletes)
- [ ] E2E flow test: test failure sets red phase, Edit triggers TDD reminder (track-test-outcome writes, tdd-reminder reads)
- [ ] E2E flow test: session-start --clear-logs resets all cross-hook state, subsequent hooks start clean
- [ ] E2E flow test: full commit flow via cape commit (detect, check, validate format, stage, commit)
- [ ] All existing hook test cases pass against new CLI implementations
- [ ] track-pr-confirmation has full test suite before migration
- [ ] cape binary installable via pnpm and available in PATH
- [ ] Old hooks removed, hooks.json points at cape hook commands
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
- [ ] Skill updates documented as follow-up (6+ skills reference prose that CLI now enforces)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO intent classification in CLI (reason: routing is an LLM task)
- NO wrapping br for CRUD operations (reason: br works fine as-is)
- NO commit grouping logic (reason: grouping requires LLM judgment)
- NO workflow state machine (reason: chain transitions need conversational context)
- NO bun dependency (reason: Node is universal)
- NO assuming uniform hook protocol (reason: 5 input shapes, 3 output shapes)
- NO dropping enforcement guards (reason: Claude may bypass after context compression)
- NO tsup for bundling (reason: unmaintained; use Vite+ tsdown)
- NO hook entry points that exit non-zero on errors (reason: hooks must fail-open with exit 0; use Effect.catchAllDefect wrapping distinct from CLI command error handling)
- NO hook logic modules inside cli/src/ (reason: hooks have a different runtime model — stdin/stdout protocol, always exit 0 — than CLI commands which use non-zero exits)

## Durable decisions
- Runtime: Node + Effect v4 + Vite+ toolchain (no bun)
- Package manager: pnpm
- CLI framework: effect/unstable/cli with Command.make and withSubcommands
- Platform services: @effect/platform-node
- Binary name: cape, in PATH
- Hook dispatcher: receives event type + matcher as CLI args, routes internally
- Hook event names: accept both PascalCase (PreToolUse) and kebab-case (pre-tool-use)
- Hook session-start: --clear-logs flag replaces CAPE_CLEAR_LOGS=1 env var
- Hook entry points: always exit 0 via Effect.catchAllDefect wrapping (fail-open)
- Hook logic placement: repo root /hooks/, not inside cli/src/ (different runtime model)
- Dual role: validated commands AND bypass guards in same binary
- Br interaction: validate + design commands only
- Output: JSON to stdout, errors to stderr (human-readable on TTY via isTTY detection)
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation/domain failure, 2 usage error
- Protocol module: protocol.ts owns all input/output type definitions
- Cutover: hard, with migrated + new test coverage
- Development: strict TDD
- E2E flow tests: spawn cape hook commands in sequence with real temp git repos and context/ dirs, verifying cross-hook state propagation
- Reference: greppa/main patterns
- Task count: 10 tasks (T1 scaffolding, T2a detect, T2b detect --map, T3 check, T4a commit, T4b br, T5 pr, T6a hooks session/prompt, T6b hooks pre-tool-use, T6c hooks post-tool-use, T7 cutover)
- Post-cutover follow-up: update 6+ skills to reference CLI commands instead of prose enforcement

## Approach
Build a cape CLI as a single pnpm package under cli/. Effect services provide testable domain logic. Commands are thin wiring via effect/unstable/cli. Hook logic stays at repo root /hooks/ with shared pure functions importable from cli/. A protocol module defines all hook input/output shapes as the single source of truth.

## Architecture
cli/src/
  index.ts             — entry: NodeServices.layer + NodeRuntime.runMain
  main.ts              — Command.make('cape') + withSubcommands
  commands/            — 9 modules (detect, check, git, commit, pr, br, hook, validate, context-internal)
  services/            — 8 Effect services (Git, Br, Detect, Check, BrValidate, GitGuard, Tdd, Context, Project)
  protocol.ts          — 5 input interfaces, 3 output constructors (denyWith, approveWith, addContext)
hooks/                 — 9 hook logic modules (repo root, not inside cli/src/)

Services:
- Git: spawn git, detect branch, check status
- Br: spawn br, query flow state, derive phase
- Project: detect repo root via .git/.beads walk-up, resolve plugin root
- Context: read/write all cross-hook state files (tdd-state.json, br-show-log.txt, pr-confirmed.txt)
- BrValidate: br create/update rule enforcement (rules 1-6, 13 from enforce-commands)
- GitGuard: git staging and PR creation guards (rules 7-12 from enforce-commands)
- Check: run detected tests, lint, format — returns structured pass/fail
- Tdd: test-file pattern detection, phase tracking, state read/write

Cross-hook state files (managed by Context service):
- hooks/context/tdd-state.json — written by track-test-outcome, read by tdd-reminder, cleared by session-start
- hooks/context/br-show-log.txt — written by track-br-show, read by enforce-commands rule 2, cleared by session-start
- hooks/context/pr-confirmed.txt — written/deleted by track-pr-confirmation, read/deleted by enforce-commands rule 12
design:
## E2E flow tests (2026-03-27)

Unit tests verify each hook in isolation. Protocol tests verify output shapes. E2E flow tests verify the cross-hook state chains that make the system work as a whole.

### Why unit tests are insufficient for hooks
The hook system has 3 cross-boundary state chains where one hook writes a file and a different hook reads it later. A unit test can pass for both sides independently while missing: wrong file path, wrong JSON shape, wrong timestamp format, stale state from a prior test. These are integration-level bugs that only surface when hooks run in sequence against shared state.

### Flow test design
Each flow test creates a real temp git repo with a real context/ directory. It spawns cape hook commands in sequence, simulating the event order Claude Code produces. Assertions verify the final observable outcome, not intermediate state.

**Flow 1: br show then br update --design**
1. Spawn `cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Bash` with stdin `{ tool_input: { command: "br show cape-abc" } }`
2. Assert br-show-log.txt contains "cape-abc"
3. Spawn `cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Bash` with stdin `{ tool_input: { command: "br update cape-abc --design '## New'" } }`
4. Assert exit 0 + empty stdout (allowed)
5. Without step 1, assert step 3 produces deny

**Flow 2: PR confirmation gate with TTL**
1. Spawn `cape hook post-tool-use --matcher AskUserQuestion` with stdin containing a PR-related question and confirming answer
2. Assert pr-confirmed.txt exists with a recent timestamp
3. Spawn `cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Bash` with stdin containing a PR creation command
4. Assert exit 0 + empty stdout (allowed)
5. Overwrite pr-confirmed.txt with an 11-minute-old timestamp
6. Repeat step 3, assert deny with expiry reason

**Flow 3: TDD red-green-refactor cycle**
1. Spawn `cape hook post-tool-use-failure --matcher Bash` with stdin containing a test command (TDD_PHASE=red env)
2. Assert tdd-state.json has phase "red"
3. Spawn `cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Edit` with stdin containing a production file path
4. Assert additionalContext contains TDD reminder
5. Spawn `cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Bash` with stdin containing a test command (phase defaults to green)
6. Assert tdd-state.json has phase "green"
7. Repeat step 3, assert no TDD reminder (fresh green state)

**Flow 4: Session start clears stale state**
1. Write stale data to br-show-log.txt, tdd-state.json, pr-confirmed.txt
2. Spawn `cape hook session-start --clear-logs`
3. Assert all three files are cleared/deleted
4. Subsequent hook spawns start with clean state

**Flow 5: Full commit pipeline**
1. In temp git repo with staged changes
2. Run `cape commit file.ts -m "feat: add thing"`
3. Assert exit 0, git log shows the commit
4. Run `cape commit file.ts -m "bad message"`
5. Assert exit 1, no commit created
6. Run `cape commit . -m "feat: bulk"`
7. Assert exit 1, bulk staging rejected

### Test infrastructure
- Temp git repo per test (git init + initial commit)
- Real context/ directory under temp root
- CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT pointed at temp root
- Subprocess spawning via Node child_process (not Effect layers — these are binary-level tests)
- Cleanup via afterEach rm -rf

### Where these live
`cli/src/__e2e__/` or `cli/src/flows.test.ts` — separate from unit tests, can be tagged for slower CI runs if needed. Run as part of the migration acceptance gate before cutover.

title: Task 2a: cape detect command — ecosystem detection from CWD
description:
## Goal
Implement `cape detect` that inspects the current working directory and outputs JSON describing the detected ecosystem: language, test framework, linter, and formatter.

## Execution mode
AUTO (patterns established in T1, no ambiguity)

## Behaviors
- cape detect outputs JSON with `language` field (typescript, go, lua, python, rust)
- cape detect outputs JSON with `testFramework` field (vitest, jest, go-test, busted, pytest, cargo-test, null)
- cape detect outputs JSON with `linter` field (oxlint, eslint, golangci-lint, luacheck, ruff, clippy, null)
- cape detect outputs JSON with `formatter` field (oxfmt, prettier, gofmt, stylua, ruff, rustfmt, null)
- Detection uses file presence heuristics (package.json, go.mod, .stylua.toml, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml)
- When multiple signals conflict, language with strongest signal wins
- cape detect returns exit code 1 with error JSON when no ecosystem detected
- DetectService is an Effect service injectable via Layer for testing
- cape --help lists detect subcommand

## Detection heuristics (per ecosystem)
- **TypeScript**: package.json with typescript dep or tsconfig.json
  - vitest: vitest in devDeps or vite.config with test
  - jest: jest in devDeps
  - oxlint: .oxlintrc.json or oxlint in devDeps
  - eslint: .eslintrc* or eslint in devDeps
  - oxfmt: .oxfmtrc.json or oxfmt in devDeps
  - prettier: .prettierrc* or prettier in devDeps
- **Go**: go.mod present
  - go-test (built-in), golangci-lint (.golangci.yml), gofmt (built-in)
- **Lua**: .stylua.toml or .luacheckrc or init.lua
  - busted: .busted or spec/ directory, luacheck: .luacheckrc, stylua: .stylua.toml
- **Python**: pyproject.toml or setup.py or requirements.txt
  - pytest: pytest in deps, ruff: ruff in deps or ruff.toml
- **Rust**: Cargo.toml present
  - cargo-test (built-in), clippy (built-in), rustfmt (built-in)

## References
- cli/src/services/git.ts — Effect service pattern from T1
- cli/src/services/gitLive.ts — live layer pattern from T1
- cli/src/commands/gitContext.ts — command wiring pattern from T1

- [ ] Correctly detects Lua ecosystem (.stylua.toml present)

- [ ] Correctly detects Rust ecosystem (Cargo.toml present)

title: Task 6b: cape hook pre-tool-use — Bash and Skill matchers
description:
## Goal
Add pre-tool-use event handling to the cape hook dispatcher. Migrates enforce-commands (Bash matcher) and flow-gate (Skill matcher) logic into the CLI HookService as pure, testable functions.

## Execution mode
AUTO

## Behaviors
### cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Bash
- Reads command from stdin JSON (tool_input.command)
- Applies all 13 enforcement guards from hooks/enforce-commands.ts:
  1. --design on br create → deny (use --description)
  2. br update --design without prior br show → deny (reads br-show-log.txt)
  3. br create missing --type or --priority → deny
  4. br create description header validation per type (task needs Goal/Behaviors/Success criteria, bug needs Reproduction steps or Evidence, epic needs Requirements/Success criteria)
  5. --status in-progress (hyphen) or --status done on br update → deny
  6. Bulk git staging (dot or -A/--all) → deny
  7. PR creation from default branch → deny
  8. PR creation with uncommitted changes → deny
  9. PR creation --body with invented sections → deny
  10. PR creation without pr-confirmed.txt or expired TTL → deny (consumes confirmation)
  11. br create missing --labels → deny
  12. stop-reinforcement: br close command → output additionalContext with stop message
- Accumulates violations and denies with combined reason
- Git commands (branch check, status check) use HookService.spawnGit
- Pass-through: empty stdout + exit 0

### cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Skill
- Reads skill name from stdin JSON (tool_input.skill)
- Gates execute-plan: requires open epic + ready tasks
- Gates finish-epic: requires all tasks under epic closed
- Gates fix-bug: requires open bug
- Uses HookService.brQuery for br checks
- Non-gated skills pass through
- br unavailability → allow through (fail-open)

### Protocol output
- Deny: { hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: string } }
- Approve with context: { additionalContext: string } (for stop-reinforcement on br close)
- Pass-through: empty stdout

## References
- hooks/enforce-commands.ts — existing Bash matcher logic (13 rules)
- hooks/enforce-commands.test.ts — 26 existing test cases to migrate
- hooks/flow-gate.ts — existing Skill matcher logic
- hooks/flow-gate.test.ts — 14 existing test cases to migrate
- hooks/stop-reinforcement.ts — br close additionalContext injection
- hooks/stop-reinforcement.test.ts — 6 existing test cases to migrate
- hooks/io.ts — deny() output format (protocol reference)
- hooks/paths.ts — context file paths (br-show-log, pr-confirmed)
- cli/src/services/hook.ts — HookService interface to extend
- cli/src/commands/hook.ts — command to extend with PreToolUse case

## Success criteria
- [ ] cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Bash enforces all 13 command guards
- [ ] cape hook pre-tool-use --matcher Skill gates execute-plan, finish-epic, fix-bug
- [ ] Non-gated skills pass through
- [ ] br unavailability allows through (fail-open)
- [ ] stop-reinforcement injects additionalContext on br close
- [ ] Deny output matches protocol envelope shape
- [ ] Pass-through is empty stdout + exit 0
- [ ] HookService extended with spawnGit, readContextFile, writeContextFile, removeContextFile
- [ ] All 46 existing test cases migrated (26 enforce-commands + 14 flow-gate + 6 stop-reinforcement)
- [ ] All existing tests still passing
- [ ] vp check passes

Pure functions: checkBrRules, checkGitStagingRules, checkPrBodyRules, checkStopReinforcement. Effect functions: checkBrShowRequirement, checkPrCreationGuards, preToolUseBash, preToolUseSkill. All 13 enforcement guards + 3 flow gates + stop-reinforcement migrated. vp check clean. .prettierignore updated to exclude TS/JS files.

title: Task 6c: cape hook post-tool-use and post-tool-use-failure — all matchers
description:
## Goal
Add PostToolUse and PostToolUseFailure event handling to the cape hook dispatcher. Migrates track-br-show (Bash), track-test-outcome (Bash + failure), track-pr-confirmation (AskUserQuestion), and tdd-reminder (Edit) logic into the CLI HookService as pure, testable functions.

## Execution mode
AUTO

## Behaviors
### cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Bash
- Reads command from stdin JSON (tool_input.command)
- Detects br show commands via regex, appends bead ID to br-show-log.txt
- Detects test commands (bun test, npm test, vitest, pytest, go test, cargo test, busted, python -m pytest/unittest), writes tdd-state.json with phase green
- Side-effect only: no stdout output, exit 0
- Creates context dir if missing
- Invalid/empty input: exit 0 silently

### cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Edit
- Reads file path from stdin JSON (tool_input.file_path)
- Skips test files (.test.ts, _test.go, _spec.lua, test_*.py, __tests__/)
- Skips non-code files (only .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.go/.py/.rs/.lua are code files)
- Checks flow context via queryFlowState/deriveFlowContext — skips if not executing or debugging
- Reads tdd-state.json — skips if fresh red state (< 10 min TTL)
- Outputs { additionalContext: "TDD reminder..." } when reminder needed
- No output when skipped, exit 0

### cape hook post-tool-use --matcher AskUserQuestion
- Reads questions/answers from stdin JSON (tool_input.questions[], tool_input.answers{})
- Checks if any question text matches PR keywords (/\bpr\b|pull request/i)
- Non-PR questions: exit 0 silently
- Confirmed: writes Date.now() to pr-confirmed.txt
- Rejected (cancel/abort/edit/revise): deletes pr-confirmed.txt
- Creates context dir if missing

### cape hook post-tool-use-failure --matcher Bash
- Reads command from stdin JSON (tool_input.command)
- Detects test commands (same pattern as PostToolUse Bash)
- Writes tdd-state.json with phase red (distinct from green in PostToolUse)
- Non-test commands: exit 0 silently

### General
- All handlers always exit 0 (fail-open)
- PostToolUseFailure is a distinct event type, not merged with PostToolUse
- Pure helper functions: isTestCommand, isTestFile, isCodeFile reused from hooks/tdd.ts patterns
- Context file paths: hooks/context/br-show-log.txt, hooks/context/tdd-state.json, hooks/context/pr-confirmed.txt

## Success criteria

- [ ] cape hook post-tool-use --matcher Bash tracks test outcomes (writes tdd-state.json with green phase)

Pure helpers: isTestFile, isTestCommand, isCodeFile with 28 unit tests. postToolUseBash: tracks br show (writes br-show-log.txt) + test outcomes (writes tdd-state.json green), 6 tests. postToolUseEdit: TDD reminder with flow context, TTL, stale state handling, 14 tests. postToolUseAskUserQuestion: PR confirmation write/delete with keyword detection, 9 tests. postToolUseFailureBash: red phase TDD state tracking, 7 tests. title: Epic: Hook-based workflow flow enforcement
description:
## Requirements
- PreToolUse hook on Skill tool denies `execute-plan` when no open epic or no ready tasks exist (br state check)
- PreToolUse hook on Skill tool denies `finish-epic` when open tasks remain under the epic (br state check)
- PreToolUse hook on Skill tool denies `fix-bug` when no bug exists in br (br state check)
- Only three skills are phase-gated: execute-plan, finish-epic, fix-bug. All other skills pass through ungated
- UserPromptSubmit hook injects chain-position context ("you are in phase X") on every user message, derived from br state
- SessionStart hook injects phase context on startup, resume, clear, and compact events
- PostToolUse hook on Bash detects `br close` commands and injects additionalContext telling the LLM to stop — no tool denial
- Deny messages include the correct skill to load instead
- br is the single source of truth — no separate state files or CLI tools
- br unavailability defaults to permissive (never deny based on absence)
- Existing hook behavior preserved (TDD injection, edit tracking, command enforcement, gentle reminders)

## Durable decisions
- Gate checks use br CLI directly via Bun.spawnSync, same pattern as existing user-prompt-submit.ts TDD check
- Deny response shape matches enforce-commands.ts: { hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName, permissionDecision, permissionDecisionReason } }
- No output = allow, matching existing hook convention
- Task closure detection regex targets `br close` (not `br update --status closed`)
- Chain-position injection wrapped in `<flow-context>` tags for distinguishability
- Brainstorm remains side-effect-free; brainstorm→write-plan stays soft-enforced via conversation context

## Anti-patterns
- NO separate state files (reason: creates two sources of truth that drift from br)
- NO blanket tool denial / pausePending mechanism (reason: deadlocks skills that need tools to finish gracefully)
- NO PostToolUse/Skill for phase advancement (reason: fires at skill LOAD not COMPLETION, would deadlock every skill invocation)
- NO gating of read-only tools (reason: blocking them creates deadlocks for no benefit)
- NO gating of write-plan or brainstorm (reason: write-plan creates epics, gating it blocks multi-epic workflows; brainstorm is side-effect-free by design)
- NO cape-flow CLI tool (reason: br is already the state machine; parallel CLI adds unnecessary concepts)
- NO restructuring brainstorm to produce br artifacts (reason: enforcement should serve the workflow, not the other way around)

## Success criteria
- [ ] execute-plan denied when no open epic exists
- [ ] execute-plan denied when no ready tasks exist
- [ ] finish-epic denied when open tasks remain under the epic
- [ ] fix-bug denied when no bug exists in br
- [ ] Non-gated skills (commit, review, beads, branch, brainstorm, write-plan) always allowed
- [ ] Deny messages include the correct skill to load instead
- [ ] Chain-position context injected on every user message via UserPromptSubmit
- [ ] Phase context injected on session start/resume/clear/compact via SessionStart
- [ ] br close in Bash triggers stop-reinforcement additionalContext
- [ ] br unavailability defaults to allow (no crashes)
- [ ] Existing hook behavior preserved
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing
design:

**Completed:** 2026-03-26
**Tasks:** 3 tasks completed
**Summary:** Built three-layer flow enforcement via Claude Code hooks: (1) flow-gate.ts PreToolUse/Skill hook denies execute-plan, finish-epic, and fix-bug when br prerequisites are unmet; (2) flow-context injection in UserPromptSubmit and SessionStart hooks tells the LLM its current phase (idle/planning/executing/debugging); (3) stop-reinforcement.ts PostToolUse/Bash hook injects additionalContext on br close to halt forward progress. All layers default to permissive when br is unavailable. No separate state files — br is the single source of truth.
**Verification:** All 109 hook tests passing, all 13 success criteria met with evidence, pre-commit hooks clean.

title: Task 1: Phase gate hook (flow-gate.ts)
description:
## Goal
Create the PreToolUse/Skill hook that denies phase-gated skill invocations when br state prerequisites are not met. This is the core enforcement mechanism — the hardest gate in the system.

## Execution mode
HITL — first hook gating the Skill tool, needs human review of deny logic and message wording.

## Behaviors
- Parses tool_input.skill from stdin JSON on PreToolUse/Skill events
- Handles gracefully if tool_input.skill is missing or has unexpected format (log warning, allow)
- Allows non-gated skills immediately (exit 0, no output)
- Denies execute-plan when `br list --type epic --status open` returns empty
- Denies execute-plan when `br ready` returns empty (epic exists but no ready tasks)
- Denies finish-epic by querying all open epics and checking each for open children; denies if any epic has open tasks remaining

title: Task 2: Chain-position context injection (UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart)
description:
## Goal
Inject flow-context tags into every user message (UserPromptSubmit) and on session events (SessionStart) so the LLM knows the current workflow phase derived from br state.

## Context
Task 1 built flow-gate.ts (PreToolUse/Skill deny logic). This task adds the soft-enforcement layer: context injection that tells the LLM what phase it's in without blocking any tools.

## Execution mode
HITL

## Behaviors
- UserPromptSubmit hook queries br state and injects `<flow-context>` tag with current phase (idle, planning, executing, debugging)
- Phase derivation: no open epic = idle; open epic + no in-progress task = planning; in-progress task = executing; open bug = debugging
- SessionStart hook injects same `<flow-context>` on startup, resume, clear, and compact events
- Existing UserPromptSubmit behavior preserved (beads skill detection, TDD enforcement)
- Existing SessionStart behavior preserved (don-cape skill injection, br-show-log clearing)
- br unavailability = skip injection (no crash, no additionalContext about phase)
- `<flow-context>` tags are distinguishable from other injected context

## References
- hooks/user-prompt-submit.ts (existing beads + TDD injection)
- hooks/session-start.ts (existing don-cape injection)
- hooks/flow-gate.ts (br query pattern via Bun.spawnSync)

- [ ] SessionStart injects `<flow-context>` on startup/resume/clear/compact
- [ ] Phase derivation correctly maps br state to idle/planning/executing/debugging
- [ ] Existing UserPromptSubmit behavior preserved (beads, TDD)
